reviews.review_title: When Action Packed Lemings meet VR....
reviews.review_description:
Update from version 2.0: Man... MR looks great in Bridge the Gap! Also Fitness mode is fun! and nice touch to add chill out difficulty level. This is big update and certainly the game is better now and refreshed quite a lot (also you can easily change language right now if required).

Old post:
Ok core idea looks a lot like old PC game Lemmings. Little Lemmings... I mean Monks slowly start to show up from one point and with skills, tricks and wits you have to help them to get to exit. The more You save, the bigger points you get (and stars ranking similiar like in mobile apps) and this unlocks next levels which require you to do the same task in more challenging setting. From here the similarities ends - Instead of ordering your Monks to do the job themselves to get to the other side you need to be fast and help them by placing square or triangle like "spirit" blocks (which disintegrate after few monks pass them and you have to place them again), using your hands directly like bridge or just by grabbing them and moving a bit closer to target, so happy bunch will reach their exit. Initially this looks easy but after several tutorial levels you are starting to feel like circus freak juggling the poor guys like crazy (by the way really enjoy throwing them :-) ). This is really fun and you get the feel of "just another level and I will finish....". Overall for early access game you get quite a lot of levels and sound mechanics which will be improved with time. Definitely this game is something I can recommend not only for Lemmings fans but also to all types of people who like some puzzle/action/logical games and generally enjoy a challenge :-)

Joy when you complete a level with just one star. Unstoppable happiness when you save all the Monkies. Sadness when they fall into the abyss because you didn’t make their path in time. Fear as you wonder if you’ll manage to finish the level, with one hand forming a bridge and the other building a path from spirit blocks. Surprise when you discover a new way to beat a level.

The game is super enjoyable. The mechanics are great, and I love picking up and moving the Monkies to different places. I’m a completionist, so getting 3 stars on every level will take me a while :D. There are plenty of levels for an Early Access game— I’m only at the end of the Forest. The game has a way of pulling you in for longer sessions. Just when you think you’ll take off the headset, you tell yourself, "One more try, just one." I haven’t had this much fun in ages. The Monkies are adorable, and you just want to save them all! I highly recommend this arcade-style game to everyone.

reviews.review_title: Easy to play, hard to master
reviews.review_description:
I love this game! The idea is super simple: move monkies from one portal to another. Finishing a level isn’t too hard, but the real challenge is getting the highest score on each map. After finishing the first biome, I’m hooked and I will try unlock everything. It’s so satisfying to get 3 stars and see those cute little minions so happy for your help.

You’ve got to try this! :D

Simple yet incredibly fun! The game is absolutely addictive, especially when you're aiming to collect all the stars on every level. I really enjoy how each level can be completed in multiple ways. I absolutely love the art style, and the Monkies themselves look irresistibly adorable!
Imagine the fast paced Beat Saber mixed with tetris-style and you’ve got Monkies! Truly exceptional title on Quest!
Super casual fun!  Tests not only your reflexes but also your thinking in a beautiful, animated setup.

It reminds me of Mario blended with Synth Riders :D Totally fresh approach to casual gameplay. You need to ensure safe passage for the Monkies by patching the road with different bricks or dragging them around. In time the levels become more and more challenging and it’s getting super fun!
This game is soo pretty in terms of art style :O Reminds me of games such as Sonic or Mario Bros. Gameplay is great - I felt challenged when trying to max out stars in each level.
Monkies are super sweet as minions, you will do anything to save them!
This one is completely unique on Quest and the Monkies are super sweet, you want to pet them! I used the referral code for it and it was worth every penny, very addictive.
